item.title: Hydration breaks purely sporting, not commercial: Infantino
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has defended the introduction of hydration breaks at the World Cup, insisting that for football’s governing body they are driven purely by sporting considerations and not commercial ​interests......

item.title: Oyarzabal happy to play through pain
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal said a minor knee injury will not put his World Cup at risk after kickstarting Spain’s challenge by scoring twice in a 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia......

item.title: Bellingham rues England’s ‘second game fever’
item.description: Jude Bellingham said England suffered a recurrence of ‘second game fever’ as they were held to a disappointing 0-0 draw by Ghana at the World Cup on Tuesday......

item.title: Dalic: Croatia need to sharpen focus
item.description: Croatia must handle pressure better and sharpen their focus if they hope to make a deep run at the World Cup, coach Zlatko ​Dalic said after his team laboured to a 1-0 win over Panama on ‌Tuesday......

item.title: Munoz sends Colombia into round of 32
item.description: Daniel Munoz fired Colombia into the World Cup Round of 32 on Tuesday, sealing a 1-0 ​win over DR Congo in Group K after goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi had threatened to turn the match ‌into a night of frustration......

item.title: Nasum makes big leap in ICC T20I rankings
item.description: Bangladesh left-arm spinner Nasum Ahmed on Wednesday made an impressive leap in the ICC Men’s T20I rankings following the recently concluded three-match series against Australia......

item.title: Joty & Co face India today
item.description: Bangladesh women’s national cricket team will take on India in their Group 1 match of the ongoing ICC Women’s T20 World Cup at Old Trafford in Manchester, today......

item.title: Outlying areas need stronger dengue defence
item.description: THE increasing shift of dengue infection from the capital city to outlying areas has alarmed public health experts, who say that it would make dengue control far more difficult in the coming years if preventive measures are not taken at the earliest. Directorate General of Health Services records show 5,160 hospital admissions because of dengue infection......

item.title: Banks should not abandon their core responsibilities
item.description: A SHARP rise in banks’ investment in government securities reflects a deepening crisis of the banking sector. Banks’ investments in government securities, according to the Bangladesh Bank, increased by 35.5 per cent in 2025 to Tk 4,04,931 crore that year from Tk 2,99,001 crore a year ago. The increase indicates that banks, faced with soaring default loans and liquidity......

item.title: Investing in higher education for growth
item.description: EDUCATION is widely regarded as the backbone of a nation. From Adam Smith to Nobel laureates Milton Friedman and Kenneth Arrow, economists have consistently emphasised that education not only enriches individuals intellectually and economically but also transforms society and drives national development. However, education encompasses primary, secondary......

item.title: Saving haor means saving the future
item.description: STANDING on the Islampur Bridge at the southern edge of Kauadighi Haor in Moulvibazar, leaders and activists of the Haor Protection Movement welcomed prime minister Tarique Rahman on June 17 during his official visit to the district. They held colourful banners and chanted slogans, but the gathering was not a political demonstration. It was the heartfelt plea of......

item.title: A nation’s secrets should live under its own law
item.description: BANGLADESH has begun bringing its most sensitive data home. The task now — and the opportunity for its government — is to finish the job well......

item.title: Halala, shadow polygyny and women’s vulnerability
item.description: MANY years ago, I read a story about colonial Calcutta, in a novel Prothom Alo by Sunil Gangopadhya. A woman, once kept by men of the respectable world, was trying to admit her son to a school. The problem was not the boy’s merit, but his father’s name, since he did not have one that society could publicly accept. The mother pleaded with the principal and somehow......

item.title: Japan PM heckled at WWII memorial
item.description: Japanese prime minister Sanae Takaichi was heckled at a World War II memorial event by protesters angry about Tokyo’s further shift away from its decades-long pacifist stance, television footage showed......

item.title: Nuclear arms only guarantee against global war: Russia
item.description: Nuclear weapons are the ‘only’ bulwark preventing the world from slipping into a global war, the Kremlin said Wednesday amid fears of a fresh multi-country arms race......

item.title: Euclid telescope snaps best photo yet of Milky Way’s heart
item.description: The Euclid space telescope has captured the largest and most detailed photo ever taken of our galaxy’s crowded heart, a dazzling image packed with 60 million stars, the European Space Agency said Wednesday......

item.title: Forex reserves cross $36b after 43 months
item.description: Bangladesh’s gross foreign exchange reserves have risen above $36 billion for the first time in nearly four years, supported by strong remittance inflows and sustained dollar purchases by the Bangladesh Bank amid improving conditions in the foreign exchange market......

item.title: Mobile phone operators owe govt Tk 13,344cr
item.description: Mobile phone operators in Bangladesh owe the government a total of Tk 13,344 crore in unpaid revenues, with leading private operator Grameenphone and state-owned Teletalk among the largest defaulters......

item.title: Apex Spinning closed, affecting 5,000 workers
item.description: Apex Spinning and Knitting Mills Ltd has decided to shut down its factory in Gazipur, affecting around 5,000 employees and workers, citing ongoing labour unrest......

item.title: Govt prioritises SMEs for competitive economy
item.description: Industries, commerce, textiles and jute minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Wednesday said that the government was giving the highest priority to the small and medium enterprise sector to transform Bangladesh into an investment-friendly, competitive and employment-oriented economy......

item.title: Govt to buy 2 cargoes LNG, 60,000 tonnes fertiliser, 4cr litres edible oil
item.description: The Bangladesh government on Wednesday decided to procure two cargoes of liquefied natural gas, 60,000 tonnes of fertiliser, 4 crore litres of edible oil and 22,500 tonnes of lentil to meet the local demand......

item.title: Bangladesh seeks greater use of UK trade preferences
item.description: Bangladesh and the United Kingdom agreed to strengthen awareness and capacity-building efforts to help local exporters make greater use of the UK’s Developing Countries Trading Scheme, as the country prepares for its graduation from the least developed country status......

item.title: Trump orders probe because gas prices not falling enough
item.description: US president Donald Trump said on Wednesday he ordered an investigation into major oil companies over high gasoline prices as the US leader faces criticism over the impact of the Mideast war......

item.title: Brent falls below $75, global stocks steady
item.description: Stock markets rebounded slightly Wednesday from the previous day’s rout in technology shares, caused by concerns over massive AI spending even as borrowing costs are rising......

item.title: India hails progress in US trade talks, but no deal yet
item.description: India hailed trade talks with the United States on Wednesday as a ‘key step’ towards a deal, but stopped short of announcing a breakthrough in remaining differences......

item.title: Airbus to inspect 16 A380s after cracks found on plane wings
item.description: Airbus has announced it will inspect 16 of its A380 superjumbos, five of them immediately, after cracks were found in a key wing component on some of the jets used by the Emirates and Qantas airlines......

item.title: BGMEA seeks realistic environmental compliance roadmap
item.description: The Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association on Wednesday urged the government to formulate a science-based and realistic roadmap for environmental compliance to ensure the continued growth and global competitiveness of the country’s ready-made garment and textile sectors......

item.title: Invest Qatar connects startups to venture capital funds
item.description: Invest Qatar, an investment promotion agency of Qatar, on Wednesday announced the launch of the Venture Capital (VC) Funding Module on the Invest Qatar Gateway, developed in collaboration with Qatar Investment Authority (QIA)......

item.title: BEPZA, ILO to set up permanent EIS in EPZs
item.description: A high-level workshop was recently held at the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organisation in Turin, Italy, with the aim of establishing a permanent and sustainable employment injury scheme for workers employed in the export processing zones under the Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority......

item.title: Midland Bank holds 13th AGM
item.description: Midland Bank PLC on Wednesday held the 13th annual general meeting through a hybrid system, said a press release......

item.title: Walton Plaza launches health camps, dedicated hotline
item.description: Walton Plaza launched a dedicated healthcare hotline and organised free medical camps across more than 700 outlets nationwide to provide healthcare services to customers and the general public......

item.description: Bosnia-Herzegovina are on the verge of reaching the knockout stages of the World Cup for the first time after beating 2022 hosts Qatar 3-1 in their final Group B match on Wednesday....

item.description: Property Lifts, one of the leading lift manufacturers in Bangladesh, has received internationally recognised TÜV certification......

item.description: Deshbandhu Group on Saturday convened a meeting to exchange views in Bogura to discuss expanding business operations, enhancing production capacity and exploring new industrial investments in the region, said a press release......

item.description: A delegation from the Institute of Cost and Management Accountants of Bangladesh, led by its president Md Kausar Alam, paid a courtesy call on Bangladesh Bank governor Md Mostaqur Rahman at the BB headquarters on Monday, said a press release......

item.description: Mercantile Bank PLC on Wednesday held the 27th annual general meeting virtually, said a press release......

item.description: Prime Bank PLC has recently signed an agreement with Executive Motors Limited (BMW Bangladesh) to provide exclusive service facilities for its customers, said a press release......

item.title: Agora, foodpanda launch World Cup campaign
item.description: Online food and grocery delivery platform foodpanda, in collaboration with Agora, has launched an exclusive World Cup rewards campaign, said a press release......

item.title: DSE extends rally on bargain hunting
item.description: Stocks on Wednesday extended gaining streak for a second consecutive trading session today as investors continued to accumulate fundamentally strong and perceived undervalued shares amid expectations of market-friendly developments......

item.title: Local knowledge-based climate solutions stressed
item.description: Climate change experts, academics and activists on Wednesday stressed the need for local knowledge-based solutions to address the growing impacts of climate change, saying local communities possessed valuable experience and practices that could help build resilience against increasingly severe climate events......

item.title: 2 more die of dengue, 157 hospitalised
item.description: Bangladesh has recorded two more deaths from dengue and 157 new cases in 24 hours until 8:00am on Wednesday......

item.title: Retired teachers’ allowances within 2 years: minister
item.description: Education and primary and mass education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Wednesday said that initiatives would be taken to clear the outstanding retirement allowances of retired non-government teachers and employees, which have remained unpaid since 2022, within the next two years......

item.title: DU advances 600 steps in THE ranking 2026
item.description: The University of Dhaka strengthened its global position by 600 steps compared to past year in the impact ranking 2026 published by the United Kingdom-based Times Higher Education magazine on Wednesday......

item.description: The government is planning to build 3,500 safety shelters in the country’s 15 most lightning strike-prone districts......

item.title: Barapukuria unit 3 resumes power generation
item.description: The third unit of the coal-fired Barapukuria Thermal Power Plant in Dinajpur, with a power generation capacity of 275 megawatts, has resumed electricity generation after a 29-day shutdown......

item.title: Martyr’s father seeks justice
item.description: The father of a martyr of the 2024 July uprising on Wednesday prayed to the International Crimes Tribunal-1 to ensure punishment for those responsible for the murder of his only son Farhanul Islam Bhuiyan, better known as Farhan Fayaz, during the mass movement......

item.title: Switzerland down Canada to top Group B, both progress
item.description:
Ruben Vargas and Johan Manzambi fired Switzerland to top spot in World Cup Group B on Wednesday with a 2-1 win over Canada as the co-hosts also ensured a historic spot in the knockout rounds.
...

item.title: Refugee status not by choice, but forced upon individual: discussion
item.description: Experts, including the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees representative to Bangladesh Ivo Freijsen, said on Wednesday that refugee or stateless status is not entered into by choice but is forced upon individuals......

item.description: The government has launched an Online Pension Tracking and Management System to streamline pension services, reduce processing delay and ensure transparency in pension services for retired public servants......

item.description: The Russian House in Dhaka has organised a series of commemorative events, marking Day of Remembrance and Sorrow, in the capital......

item.title: Minister urges scientists for basic, innovative food safety research
item.description: Fisheries, livestock and agriculture minister Mohammed Amin Ur Rashid on Wednesday urged scientists to dedicate themselves to basic, innovative and quality research focusing on food nutrition, safety and public health rather than solely relying on budget allocations......

item.title: Coast Guard holds medical camp at Mongla
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard base Mongla held a medical campaign and distributed medicines among destitute people and children at Joymani High School under Mongla in Bagerhat on Wednesday......

item.title: Speedy enactment of anti-discrimination law demanded
item.description: Rights activists on Wednesday called for the urgent enactment of a comprehensive Anti-Discrimination Act, upholding the constitutional principles of equality, dignity and justice to ensure effective protection of the marginalised communities facing discrimination......

item.description: Brazil's injury-plagued football star Neymar was cheered on by loved ones and conservative presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro on Wednesday hours ahead of his long-awaited World Cup debut....

item.title: 170 Bangladeshis repatriated from Libya
item.description: One hundred and seventy more Bangladeshi nationals detained in Libya have returned home under a coordinated repatriation effort involving the Bangladesh government, Libyan authorities and the International Organization for Migration......

item.title: 3 killed in Feni, Gopalganj road accidents
item.description: At least three people were killed and two others injured in separate road accidents in Feni and Gopalganj districts on Wednesday......

item.title: Unilever holds workshop on environmental journalism
item.description: The Unilever Bangladesh Limited has recently organised a two-day workshop for students of the University of Chittagong to enhance their understanding and skills in environmental journalism......

item.title: BMU opens two-day workshop on good clinical practice
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University inaugurated a two-day workshop on good clinical practice at the Shaheed Dr Milton Hall of the university in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: Dream job: US fans paid to watch every World Cup game
item.description: It might just be the greatest job in the world. But for the two American men being paid $50,000 to watch every one of the 104 games of the World Cup over 39 days, it is a labor of love. ...

item.title: Ex-MP Momtaz shown arrested in attempted murder case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Wednesday showed former lawmaker and folk singer Momtaz Begum arrested in a case filed over an alleged attempt to murder Md Mukter Hossain during the July uprising in the capital’s Mirpur area in 2024......

item.description: An unidentified youth was beaten to death by a mob on suspicion of mugging in the Tongi area of Gazipur Wednesday morning......

item.title: Pulisic fitness boost as US seek knockout momentum against Turkey
item.description: Christian Pulisic said Wednesday he has recovered from his calf injury and the United States are eager for a third straight World Cup win against Turkey to take momentum into the knockout rounds....

item.title: Asteroid zooming past Earth on Saturday visible to stargazers
item.description: A large asteroid that will zoom harmlessly past Earth on Saturday will be visible to stargazers using a small telescope or large binoculars, the European Space Agency announced Wednesday......

item.title: 2 UPDF members killed in Khagrachari
item.description: Two members of a Chittagong Hill Tracts-based organisation, the United People’s Democratic Front, were killed in separate incidents in Ramgarh and Dighinala upazilas of Khagrachari on Wednesday......

item.title: Laporte wary of Uruguay will to avoid World Cup exit against Spain
item.description:
Spain defender Aymeric Laporte said the European champions must be wary of an onslaught from Uruguay as Marcelo Bielsa's side aim to avoid an embarrassing early World Cup exit.
...

item.title: Humid heat rise hits health, workforce hard
item.description: Bangladesh is becoming increasingly hotter and more humid, exposing millions of people to the dangerous combination of torturous heat and high humidity, threatening public health, labour productivity and urban liveability, according to two reports published recently......

item.title: AD bars dental technologists from independent practice
item.description: The Appellate Division of the Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned a High Court verdict that had allowed diploma holders in dental technology, known as dental technologists, to independently examine patients and prescribe medicines......

item.title: Europe swelters in heatwave
item.description: Europe struggled to cope with a record-breaking heatwave on Wednesday, with at least 94 million people expected to experience temperatures above 35C, most of them in France and Spain......

item.title: 4 of a family held near Dinajpur border
item.description: Four members of a family, allegedly pushed into Bangladesh by India’s Border Security Force, were detained near the Bontara border in Dinajpur Sadar upazila on Wednesday......

item.title: 10 lakh more govt officials to come under tax net
item.description: About 10 lakh more public employees are likely to come under the income tax net following the implementation of the new pay scale in the 2026–27 financial year beginning on July 1, officials said on Wednesday......

item.title: UN audit finds funds going down the drain
item.description: An internal audit has flagged mismanagement, irregularities and misuse of aid in several United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees-run Rohingya relief projects in Bangladesh......

item.title: BR plans underpasses at level crossings to ease traffic jam in Dhaka
item.description: Bangladesh Railway has begun preparing a detailed project proposal to construct underpasses at several level crossings along the busy Joydebpur-Dhaka-Narayanganj railway section in a bid to reduce traffic congestion in the capital......
